interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - maps MPE gesture values through a user-defined curve

parameterGroups.label: MPE Source
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters select the MPE gesture type and define the fallback value when no MPE data is available

parameters.name: GestureCC
parameters.description: The MPE gesture type to use for this modulator

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: The default value when no MPE data is available

parameterGroups.label: Smoothing
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters control how the raw MPE gesture value is temporally smoothed and how much the smoothed signal contributes to the output

parameters.name: SmoothingTime
parameters.description: The smoothing time for the modulation value

parameters.name: SmoothedIntensity
parameters.description: The intensity of the modulation with smoothing applied

parameterGroups.label: Voice Mode
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters control monophonic behavior and note retriggering, which are standard EnvelopeModulator parameters

parameters.name: Monophonic
parameters.description: Enables monophonic mode where only one voice is rendered

parameters.name: Retrigger
parameters.description: Restarts the envelope when a new note is triggered in monophonic mode

gaps.question: How do the 5 gesture types (Press, Slide, Glide, Stroke, Lift) map to actual MIDI CC or MPE dimensions? Press=CC74 (brightness)? Slide=CC74? Glide=pitch bend per channel? Stroke and Lift are less standard - what MIDI data do they read?
gaps.context: Users need to know which MPE dimension each gesture type corresponds to, especially since MPE spec only defines 3 dimensions (pressure, slide, pitch per-note)

gaps.question: What is the processing order? MPE gesture -> normalize -> table lookup -> smooth -> intensity scale? Where does the table fit relative to smoothing?
gaps.context: The pseudo-code must show the correct transformation chain

gaps.question: What exactly does SmoothedIntensity control? Is it a mix between the raw (unsmoothed) and smoothed signal, or a simple gain on the smoothed output? Does setting it to 0 bypass smoothing entirely?
gaps.context: The parameter name and description are ambiguous - 'intensity of modulation with smoothing applied' could mean several things

gaps.question: How does the per-voice MPE routing work? Does the module use per-channel MIDI to associate gestures with specific voices, following the MPE channel-per-note model?
gaps.context: MPE's per-note expression via per-channel routing is the key differentiator from regular CC modulators

gaps.question: Since this is an EnvelopeModulator (not TimeVariantModulator), how does the note-on/note-off lifecycle work? Does the modulation value persist after note-off, or does it release to zero?
gaps.context: EnvelopeModulator subtype implies per-voice lifecycle management that affects how the modulation behaves during release phase

gaps.question: How does Monophonic mode interact with MPE's per-note model? In monophonic mode, which channel's gesture data is used when multiple notes are active?
gaps.context: This is a potentially confusing combination - MPE is inherently polyphonic but this setting forces monophonic behavior

gaps.question: Is there a UseTable parameter or is the table always active? The base data shows no UseTable parameter unlike PitchWheel/MidiController.
gaps.context: Need to clarify how the TableProcessor interface is activated without an explicit UseTable toggle

notes: This is an EnvelopeModulator (per-voice) unlike PitchWheel/MidiController which are TimeVariantModulators (monophonic). This makes sense because MPE is inherently per-note. The 5 gesture types (Press/Slide/Glide/Stroke/Lift) exceed the 3 standard MPE dimensions - Stroke and Lift may be HISE-specific extensions or alternative names. No UseTable parameter despite implementing TableProcessor - the table may be always-on or controlled differently. Dynamic defaults on Monophonic, GestureCC, and DefaultValue suggest these may be set programmatically. Natural seeAlso candidates: PitchWheel, MidiController, Aftertouch.
